title: Talakona
tagline: Talakona — heritage city of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Talakona is situated in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Talakona is situated in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. It is a resort with waterfalls, dense forests and wildlife. Talakona waterfall is in Sri Venkateswara National Park, Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh. With a 270 feet (82 m)-fall, Talakona is the highest waterfall in Andhra Pradesh state.

topPlaces.name: Siddheswara Swamy Temple
topPlaces.description: Siddheswara Swamy Temple is a large Hindu temple located in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is located in Talakona in the Sri Venkateswara National Park of Tirupati district.

topPlaces.name: Nerabailu
topPlaces.description: Nerabailu is a village in Yerravaripalem Mandal of Tirupati district.

items.note: Siddheswara Swamy Temple is a large Hindu temple located in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is located in Talakona in the Sri Venkateswara National Park of Tirupati district.
Nerabailu is a village in Yerravaripalem Mandal of Tirupati district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Talakona?
faq.a: The best time to visit Talakona is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Tirupati Airport (41 km) and the nearest railway station is Tirupati Railway Station (30 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Talakona?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Talakona at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Talakona?
faq.a: Stays in Talakona start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Talakona?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siddheswara Swamy Temple, Nerabailu.
